reflecting the benign nature of the code.\n\n---\n\n### Confirming the code's content and security implications:\n\n**Code review:**\n\n```javascript\nimport { requireNativeComponent } from 'react-native';\nexport const FastShadowView = requireNativeComponent('FastShadowView');\n//# sourceMappingURL=FastShadowView.js.map\n```\n\n- The code imports a React Native API to load a native component.\n- It exports a constant referencing a native UI component named `'FastShadowView'`.\n- The source map comment is typical for development builds and does not impact security.\n\n**Security implications:**\n\n- The code does not process user input, handle untrusted data, or perform network operations.\n- It simply links to a native component, which is a common pattern.\n- No obfuscation, dynamic code execution, or suspicious patterns are present.\n\n**Potential vulnerabilities:**\n\n- Since the code only loads and exports a native component, any security concerns would depend on the native implementation itself, which is outside the scope of this code snippet.\n- The code does not introduce any malicious logic or data leakage vectors.\n\n---\n\n### Evaluating the scores:\n\n-
